added_assumptions.label: A-frontier-active
added_assumptions.statement: There exist nuisance estimators and a tangent-cone collection such that (i) the empirical-process remainder of the AIPW exact-identification score is bounded above by 𝔖_n(P) up to logs (the upper-bound channel from current Assumption 4), (ii) the oracle Gaussian approximation holds whenever 𝔉_n(P) → 0 (current Assumption 4, second clause), and (iii) there exist active local alternatives realizing matching minimax lower bounds with constants c_R, c_{ep} > 0 at the same scale 𝔉_n(P) (current Assumption 6). The frontier 𝔉_n(P) = 𝔖_n(P) + 𝔇_n(P) is thus assumed to be simultaneously achievable from above and matched from below.
added_assumptions.source: Regime_defining consolidation of the existing §7 Assumptions 4 and 6. Theorem-split is required because the original Theorem 1 promised an unconditional characterization of when the frontier vanishes; that characterization is now explicitly given as the assumption, and the unconditional version is preserved as open Conjecture C-frontier-unconditional / C-primitive-frontier in §8.

flags.rewound_from_stage0: Case 2c (Bucket A — regime_defining): the reviewer's correctness reject states explicitly that the flagship iff frontier is encoded in Assumptions 4 (sharp nuisance empirical-process bound + oracle Gaussian approx at scale 𝔖_n) and 6 (active tangent cones / lower-bound constants realizing the same frontier). These are not latent or caveat — they ARE the kernel of the promised two-channel sharp characterization. The reviewer themselves names the deterministic split: 'replace the high-level frontier assumptions with primitive empirical-process and minimax lower-bound arguments, OR clearly demote the result to a conditional theorem whose frontier is assumed rather than discovered.' Loop counters.theorem_splits = 0, so the autonomous-safe split path is available. Per-boundary heuristic (counterexample-grade → user) does NOT apply because the conjecture is not counterexample-grade; it is over-strong-in-its-premises, which is exactly what theorem_split handles.

proposed_from.topic: Uniform-in-DGP Neyman orthogonality breakdown frontier for heterogeneous-treatment-effect DML estimators across nuisance classes parametrized by Holder smoothness and metric entropy
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest in-repo artifact remains the failed eid_dml_orth_uniform/v2 proposal, whose reviewer asked for explicit DML/LRO/ADML report summands and primitive frontier arguments. This v3 proposal keeps the narrower ExactID Riesz-localization kernel and adds a concrete growing-report-class entropy witness; doc/API.md still records no accepted ExactID proposal duplicating a Holder/entropy local Riesz critical-radius frontier.","published_axis":"The closest published papers are ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022Riesz and ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022AutoDML for local Riesz DML, plus KennedyBalakrishnanRobinsWasserman2024MinimaxCATE for Holder CATE elbows. The gap is a primitive necessary-and-sufficient first-order DML inference frontier with three independently defined channels, including a non-corner report-packing witness; Kennedy’s exact anchor is Theorem 1, Section 3.3, and Theorem 2, as verified from https://arxiv.org/abs/2203.00837 and PubMed/PMC snippets for DOI 10.1214/24-AOS2369."}
seed_list: Construct an entropy-remainder index for first-order HTE DML and conjecture it is the necessary-and-sufficient uniform Gaussian-inference frontier over Holder nuisance and entropy-indexed report classes.
Show that below the Kennedy et al. nuisance-smoothness elbow first-order DML is uniformly impossible for CATE reports and kth-order orthogonal scores are necessary at the same exponent boundary.
Characterize primitive CEINR-admissible LRO dictionaries and weights for which ATE-specialized LRO reports equal the ADML Riesz report, with a counterexample dictionary when equality fails.
Upgrade orthogonal statistical learning from excess-risk oracle inequalities to an iff frontier for simultaneous HTE confidence bands indexed by target entropy and nuisance product rates.
Define a Riesz-localization critical radius for subgroup and shrinking-neighborhood CATE reports separating root-n regularity, slower Gaussian limits, and nonregular limits.
Give a high-dimensional normality frontier for causal forests/GRF/ORF with estimated nuisances as dimension, honesty subsample size, overlap, and nuisance entropy vary together.
Characterize black-box AutoDML/Riesz learnability through a joint entropy-approximation condition for outcome and Riesz classes, with neural-net and forest witnesses.
Turn Armstrong-Kolesar smoothness/overlap finite-sample necessity into a DML critical-overlap frontier for HTE reports under bounded Holder nuisances.
Replace the failed DML/LRO/ADML spectral studentization proposal with explicit variance-report summands and a scale-free report-ratio frontier anchored to the AIPW HTE process.
Prove an open-class lower bound showing Foster-Syrgkanis orthogonal excess-risk control does not imply Gaussian HTE bands when the entropy-remainder index diverges.

seed_details.one_line: Construct an entropy-remainder index for first-order HTE DML and conjecture it is the necessary-and-sufficient uniform Gaussian-inference frontier over Holder nuisance and entropy-indexed report classes.

anchor_paper.author: Chernozhukov, Chetverikov, Demirer, Duflo, Hansen, Newey, Robins; Kennedy, Balakrishnan, Robins, Wasserman; Armstrong, Kolesar
anchor_paper.venue: Econometrics Journal / Annals of Statistics / Econometrica
anchor_paper.claim: DML gives sufficient product-rate conditions; minimax CATE and finite-sample ATE papers expose smoothness and overlap thresholds without a first-order DML iff frontier.
anchor_paper.location: ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML section 3; Kennedy et al. 2024 sections 2-4; ArmstrongKolesar2021FiniteSampleATE main finite-sample inference results

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends sufficient DML rates to necessity; non-trivial because the named entropy-remainder scalar yields an iff boundary and open-class lower-bound witnesses, not a broader assumption list.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML because their product-rate condition already gives the boundary.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: that paper gives sufficiency, not necessity, not HTE report entropy accumulation, and not an open Holder lower-bound frontier.

adversarial_referee.instance: Headline needs a named focal object beyond rate inequalities.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The focal object is the entropy-remainder index B_n(P), defined from nuisance radii and report entropy.

adversarial_referee.instance: The lower-bound necessity half may be much harder than sufficiency.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The proposal labels it Conjecture and adds active curvature plus a wavelet witness route.

adversarial_referee.instance: Lower bound could sit on a hand-tuned CATE law.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The draft includes an open wavelet/Haar witness class away from coefficient hyperplanes.

seed_details.one_line: Show that below the Kennedy et al. nuisance-smoothness elbow first-order DML is uniformly impossible for CATE reports and kth-order orthogonal scores are necessary at the same exponent boundary.

anchor_paper.author: Kennedy, Balakrishnan, Robins, Wasserman; Chernozhukov et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Higher-order/minimax CATE and first-order DML live as separate sufficient theories; the exact first-order impossibility boundary is not stated.
anchor_paper.location: Kennedy et al. 2024 sections 2-4; ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML section 3

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends first-order to higher-order; stronger because it predicts a necessity boundary where first-order scores fail, not a routine kth-order construction.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Kennedy et al. because minimax CATE rates already imply first-order DML cannot be optimal.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: minimax estimation rates do not by themselves give uniform Gaussian-inference impossibility for the first-order DML process.

adversarial_referee.instance: Only CATE is named, not BLP/group/linear HTE reports.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Patch by formulating jointly for linear CATE reports and full CATE bands.

adversarial_referee.instance: Might only prove higher-order scores are sufficient below the elbow.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Seed requires lower bounds proving first-order impossibility.

adversarial_referee.instance: Kennedy et al. may already contain the lower bound.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Their lower bounds target CATE minimax estimation, not first-order DML Gaussian inference over report classes.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize primitive CEINR-admissible LRO dictionaries and weights for which ATE-specialized LRO reports equal the ADML Riesz report, with a counterexample dictionary when equality fails.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior proposal eid_dml_orth_uniform/v2
anchor_paper.claim: Reviewer asked for primitive necessary-and-sufficient conditions on CEINR admissible dictionaries, Jacobians, and weights.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/equivalence frontier; non-trivial only if dictionaries and Jacobians are primitive, not residual-L2 tautologies.
seed_details.referee_objection: This was already tried and rejected because the rho=0 condition was tautological.
seed_details.rebuttal: The repaired seed derives dictionaries and weights from LRO first-step equations and ADML Riesz normal equations before stating equality.

adversarial_referee.instance: Could give only a sufficient CEINR class.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Seed explicitly demands necessity plus counterexample dictionary.

adversarial_referee.instance: LRO and ADML reports may not target the same normalized object.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Must lock the ATE specialization and common EIF normalization before comparison.

adversarial_referee.instance: Counterexample dictionary could be artificially rank deficient.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Require full-rank admissible dictionary with nonzero residual Riesz direction.

seed_details.one_line: Upgrade orthogonal statistical learning from excess-risk oracle inequalities to an iff frontier for simultaneous HTE confidence bands indexed by target entropy and nuisance product rates.

anchor_paper.author: Foster, Syrgkanis; Semenova, Chernozhukov
anchor_paper.claim: OSL gives entropy-controlled excess-risk bounds; DML-CATE gives simultaneous inference under high-level nuisance conditions, but no matching inference lower bound.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends risk to inference; stronger because Gaussian approximation and anti-concentration create a separate entropy frontier.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just applying empirical process CLTs to OSL scores.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: excess-risk oracle bounds do not imply multiplier-bootstrap validity or a lower bound when nuisance products accumulate over target entropy.

adversarial_referee.instance: Needs computable evaluation map for entropy threshold.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Use the entropy integral E_n and nuisance product as the computable scalar.

adversarial_referee.instance: Only the valid-band direction may be accessible.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Lower bound comes from packing the report class and aligned nuisance perturbations.

adversarial_referee.instance: Recent empirical-process papers may already prove the band frontier.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Gaps payload flags OSL as excess-risk, not Gaussian inference; checklist includes Semenova-Chernozhukov.

seed_details.one_line: Define a Riesz-localization critical radius for subgroup and shrinking-neighborhood CATE reports separating root-n regularity, slower Gaussian limits, and nonregular limits.

anchor_paper.author: Chernozhukov, Newey, Singh
anchor_paper.claim: Riesz and AutoDML work handles global/local linear functionals but does not draw a sharp uniform regular-to-nonregular transition for shrinking HTE reports.
anchor_paper.location: ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022Riesz local functional discussion; ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022AutoDML main inference conditions

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift; stronger because the Riesz norm creates a critical radius with different limit regimes.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from nonregular local functional theory because shrinking neighborhoods have diverging Riesz norms.
seed_details.rebuttal: False: the seed asks for the joint Riesz norm, nuisance product, Holder smoothness, and report entropy frontier for DML inference.

adversarial_referee.instance: Critical radius must be independent, not defined by failure.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Define it from Riesz norm growth and nuisance radii before stating limits.

adversarial_referee.instance: Subgroup, local CATE, and weighted reports should be handled jointly.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Seed explicitly names the family of local linear CATE reports.

adversarial_referee.instance: Shrinking set might have vanishing mass only.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Use smooth kernel neighborhoods with bounded conditional density as witness.

seed_details.one_line: Give a high-dimensional normality frontier for causal forests/GRF/ORF with estimated nuisances as dimension, honesty subsample size, overlap, and nuisance entropy vary together.

anchor_paper.author: Wager, Athey; Athey, Tibshirani, Wager; Oprescu, Syrgkanis, Wu
anchor_paper.claim: Forest HTE papers prove asymptotic theory under honesty and regularity assumptions, not a sharp frontier with dimension, entropy, and estimated nuisances varying jointly.
anchor_paper.location: WagerAthey2018CausalForests asymptotic normality sections; AtheyTibshiraniWager2019GRF theory sections; OprescuSyrgkanisWu2019ORF oracle-rate theorem

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends fixed-regime forest theory to growing dimension; stronger if it identifies a failure boundary where honesty plus orthogonality no longer controls bias.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is too algorithm-specific and not a CausalSmith ExactID theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: It can be framed as an inference theorem for orthogonal local estimating equations under unconfounded ExactID, not an algorithm benchmark.

adversarial_referee.instance: Needs a named focal object beyond forest assumptions.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Use a forest-normality index combining leaf diameter, subsample size, nuisance product, and entropy.

adversarial_referee.instance: GRF targets local estimating equations, ORF targets orthogonal forest moments.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: State separate primitives and compare their common HTE score limit.

adversarial_referee.instance: Recent GRF theory may cover high dimension.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Gaps payload specifically flags sharp high-dimensional frontier as unsolved.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize black-box AutoDML/Riesz learnability through a joint entropy-approximation condition for outcome and Riesz classes, with neural-net and forest witnesses.

anchor_paper.author: Chernozhukov, Newey, Singh; Farrell, Liang, Misra
anchor_paper.claim: AutoDML estimates Riesz representers with flexible learners; DNN inference gives semiparametric rates, but sharp joint learnability conditions are not characterized.
anchor_paper.location: ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022AutoDML main AutoDML conditions; FarrellLiangMisra2021DNNInference rate/inference results

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extends ADML to black-box learners; stronger if the learnability condition is necessary and sufficient and includes worked DNN/forest witnesses.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just substituting known neural-network rates into AutoDML assumptions.
seed_details.rebuttal: False if the theorem characterizes the joint outcome-Riesz entropy and approximation frontier with necessity examples.

adversarial_referee.instance: Needs an explicit bridge/Riesz function class object.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Use the learned Riesz class and its entropy-approximation envelope as the focal object.

adversarial_referee.instance: May only give sufficient black-box conditions.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Seed requires lower-bound examples where either outcome or Riesz class violates the condition.

adversarial_referee.instance: Neural-net example could be approximation-only without stochastic error.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Demand separate approximation and estimation terms for ReLU networks and forests.

seed_details.one_line: Turn Armstrong-Kolesar smoothness/overlap finite-sample necessity into a DML critical-overlap frontier for HTE reports under bounded Holder nuisances.

anchor_paper.author: Armstrong, Kolesar; Chernozhukov et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Finite-sample ATE inference depends on smoothness, shape, and overlap; DML sufficient conditions usually fix overlap away from zero.
anchor_paper.location: ArmstrongKolesar2021FiniteSampleATE main results; ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML overlap assumptions

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Sensitivity to overlap violation; stronger if it gives a critical-overlap scalar for sign or coverage failure, not just weaker overlap.
seed_details.referee_objection: Weak overlap DML frontiers are already standard through inverse propensity variance blow-up.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposed gap is the joint Holder/entropy/overlap critical scalar for HTE report uniform Gaussian inference.

adversarial_referee.instance: Critical-overlap scalar may be less central than entropy frontier.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Could be field-tier unless tied to HTE entropy and lower-bound witnesses.

adversarial_referee.instance: May only show wider variance under weak overlap.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Needs critical overlap at which Gaussian studentization fails uniformly.

adversarial_referee.instance: Armstrong-Kolesar may not be DML-specific.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Use it as necessity precedent; DML comparator remains Chernozhukov et al.

seed_details.one_line: Replace the failed DML/LRO/ADML spectral studentization proposal with explicit variance-report summands and a scale-free report-ratio frontier anchored to the AIPW HTE process.

anchor_paper.author: CausalSmith prior proposal eid_dml_orth_uniform/v2; Chernozhukov et al.
anchor_paper.venue: CausalSmith failed banked proposal / Econometrics Journal
anchor_paper.claim: Prior proposal tried a spectral studentization frontier but reviewers found DML/LRO/ADML variance summands and scale-free normalization missing.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Decompose-then-correct/report frontier; non-trivial only if explicit report summands replace the failed spectral placeholder.
seed_details.referee_objection: This re-proposes a burned prior angle.
seed_details.rebuttal: It should be dropped unless rewritten around explicit summands; not chosen for v1 to avoid duplication risk.

seed_details.one_line: Prove an open-class lower bound showing Foster-Syrgkanis orthogonal excess-risk control does not imply Gaussian HTE bands when the entropy-remainder index diverges.

anchor_paper.author: Foster, Syrgkanis; Semenova, Chernozhukov
anchor_paper.claim: Orthogonal statistical learning controls excess risk, while DML-CATE performs simultaneous inference; the implication from risk to bands is not automatic.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/impossibility; stronger because it targets a named inference shortcut and promotes to an open class via entropy-remainder divergence.
seed_details.referee_objection: No one claimed OSL excess-risk bounds imply confidence bands, so this may be a strawman.
seed_details.rebuttal: Frame as a bridge frontier rather than refuting Foster-Syrgkanis; the consumer is HTE band construction from orthogonal pseudo-outcomes.

adversarial_referee.instance: Foster-Syrgkanis do not promise Gaussian bands.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Do not say refutes them; say it closes the risk-to-inference bridge.

adversarial_referee.instance: Lower-bound witness could be artificial entropy explosion.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Use polynomial entropy/Haar classes with bounded outcomes and overlap.

adversarial_referee.instance: Needs named scalar diagnosing failure.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Use the same entropy-remainder index B_n(P).

proposed_from.literature_map: Chernozhukov et al. (2018, Econometrics Journal, ChernozhukovEtAl2018DML) introduce orthogonal scores and cross-fitting with sufficient product-rate conditions, but no necessity frontier. Semenova and Chernozhukov (2021, Econometrics Journal, SemenovaChernozhukov2021DMLCATE) give DML simultaneous inference for CATE summaries under high-level conditions, but not a Holder/entropy lower-bound frontier. Foster and Syrgkanis (2023, Annals of Statistics, FosterSyrgkanis2023OSL) give orthogonal statistical learning excess-risk bounds under metric entropy, but not Gaussian inference breakdown. Chernozhukov, Newey, and Singh (2022, Econometrica, ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022AutoDML) automate debiasing through learned Riesz representers, raising learnability-frontier questions. Chernozhukov, Newey, and Singh (2022, Econometrics Journal, ChernozhukovNeweySingh2022Riesz) distinguish global and local functionals where Riesz norms can diverge. Kennedy et al. (2024, Annals of Statistics, KennedyBalakrishnanRobinsWasserman2024MinimaxCATE) derive minimax CATE rates and nuisance-smoothness elbows using higher-order ideas. Armstrong and Kolesar (2021, Econometrica, ArmstrongKolesar2021FiniteSampleATE) show honest ATE inference depends on smoothness, shape, and overlap restrictions. Wager and Athey (2018), Athey, Tibshirani, and Wager (2019), and Oprescu, Syrgkanis, and Wu (2019) provide forest/GRF/ORF HTE inference without a sharp high-dimensional normality frontier. Farrell, Liang, and Misra (2021, Econometrica) connect neural-network rates to semiparametric inference. Main tension: DML papers give sufficient orthogonality conditions, while minimax and finite-sample work expose thresholds where first-order orthogonality should fail.
